mnemonics: My Pet Chameleon Eats Only Flies (Memory Aid for the Chomsky Hierarchy: Type-0: Unrestricted, Type-1: Context-Sensitive, Type-2: Context-Free, Type-3: Regular)
keyPoints: The Chomsky Hierarchy classifies formal grammars into four types (0-3) based on their generative power and restrictions.
A Turing Machine is the automaton equivalent to a Type-0 (Unrestricted) grammar, capable of recognizing a recursively enumerable language.
The Pumping Lemma is a tool used to prove that certain languages are not regular or not context-free.
commonMistakes: Assuming all languages are decidable or recognizable; many are not, such as the Halting Problem.
Confusing the capabilities of different automata (e.g., thinking a PDA can solve problems requiring a Turing Machine).
Misapplying the Pumping Lemma by using it to try and prove a language IS regular or context-free, when it can only be used to prove a language is NOT.
